methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series G → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.25x) to $75M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Infrastructure & Logistics (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 1 same-sector peers exist in Infrastructure & Logistics, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Earth AI, a Series G infrastructure and logistics company founded by Roman Teslyuk, presents a limited comparable universe for triangulation. The private comp set contains only one peer (AiDash), with no public comparables identified in the sector. This data scarcity constrains the application of peer-median methods; the single private peer is insufficient to establish a reliable cohort benchmark without risking undue influence from idiosyncratic factors.

Stage-progression analysis is the primary applicable method here. Using total capital raised of $20M as a proxy baseline, normalized to $75M, and applying a 1.25x step-up multiplier typical for Series G-stage advancement, the method implies a valuation of $94M. This reflects the expectation that Earth AI would command a modest multiple over its cumulative capital deployment as it approaches later-stage funding or exit.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $80M to $108M, centered at $94M. The narrow band—approximately ±15% of median—reflects the constraints of single-method triangulation rather than genuine certainty. Key uncertainties include the appropriateness of the 1.25x step-up multiplier for this specific company's capital efficiency and growth trajectory, and the complete absence of sector-specific peer data to validate stage-based assumptions. Investors should treat this range as provisional pending acquisition of broader Infrastructure & Logistics comparables or additional company-specific operating metrics.
